You will be the technical solution developer of our agentic automation initiatives, responsible for designing, building, and governing autonomous AI agents that leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) and a suite of digital tools.
   

  

 This is more than a development role; you are a pioneer in a rapidly emerging field. You will design and orchestrate multi-tool AI agents capable of handling dynamic, goal-oriented work. While your expertise will be rooted in modern AI development with Python and agentic frameworks, your ability to integrate these agents with our existing enterprise platforms like Power Automate and UiPath will be critical.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities:**
* Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify high-value opportunities for agentic automation where autonomous agents can manage complex, goal-driven objectives.
* Translate high-level business goals into agentic workflows, defining agent objectives, toolsets, and success metrics.


*Agent Design & Development:*
* Lead the design and development of sophisticated, autonomous AI agents using agentic frameworks and LLMs.
* Develop end-to-end agentic solutions, equipping agents with tools to interact with enterprise applications via APIs, UI automation, and other integrations using products eg., n8n.
* Develop and maintain a library of reusable agent skills, tools, and prompt templates to accelerate future development and enhance agent capabilities.


*Platform & Tool Governance:*
* Establish governance for the agentic automation ecosystem, including LLM usage, prompt management, vector databases, and ensuring responsible AI principles.
* Contribute to the administration of our existing automation platforms, such as Power Automate and UiPath, ensuring they can be leveraged as tools by AI agents.


*Technical Leadership & Mentorship:*
* Serve as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) for Agentic AI solutions, LLMs, and prompt engineering, providing guidance to the wider organization.
* Champion best practices in agentic design, including robust planning, reasoning, error handling, and security.
* Conduct workshops and code reviews to uplift the technical capabilities of the automation community at Tele2.


**Required Skills & Qualifications (Must-Haves):**
* 3 to 5 years of hands-on experience in software consulting, development and/or automation, with a portfolio in AI/ML or agentic systems.
* Good-level proficiency in Power Automate and/or UiPath with hands-on experience with agentic AI frameworks and Claude Code.
* Good understanding of Large Language Models (LLMs) and experience integrating with them via APIs (e.g., Azure OpenAI).
* Strong knowledge of prompt engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and vector databases.
* Strong understanding of API-based integration (REST/SOAP) and data formats like JSON.
* Excellent problem-solving skills, with the ability to deconstruct complex business goals into logical, agent-executable plans.
* A bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, or a related field.


**Preferred Skills & Qualifications (Nice-to-Haves):**
* Microsoft Certifications, particularly in Azure AI or Power Platform (e.g., PL-500).
* Familiarity with Azure AI services, including Azure Functions and Azure Logic Apps.
* Experience with MLOps, deploying AI models and systems into production environments.
* Experience working in an Agile/Scrum development environment.


**Benefits**
 Pension and health care
   

  

 Hybrid and flexible work life balance
   

  

 Career opportunities
   

  

 Parental benefit
   

  

 Employee benefits and Tele2 discounts
   

  

 Inclusive workplace

title: Information Architect
description:
**I** 
**nformationsarkitekter till B3** 
**Visab Consulting**
 Hos B3 Visab Consulting stödjer vi finansindustrins utveckling i den digitala ekonomin. Vi söker nu fler engagerade informationsarkitekter som vill vara med och bidra till en samhällskritisk bransch. Med över 40 års erfarenhet har vi byggt långsiktiga relationer med ledande aktörer inom bank och försäkring, och vår expertis gör oss även till en attraktiv partner för snabbväxande företag.
   

  

**Om rollen** 
 Som informationsarkitekt hos oss får du arbeta i varierande uppdrag där du hjälper kunder att utveckla och implementera robust informationsarkitektur. Du kommer att:
   

  

* Skapa riktlinjer och standarder för informationsarkitekturens struktur.
* Sätta upp processer för att hantera och vidareutveckla informationsflöden.
* Säkerställa att rätt information når rätt personer vid rätt tidpunkt.
* Arbeta med datalagring, tillgänglighet och säkerhet för att förhindra obehörig åtkomst och dataförluster.


 Vi tror att framgång bygger på samarbete och kunskapsdelning och vi ser gärna att du är en lagspelare som uppskattar att arbeta prestigelöst och flexibelt.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet som informationsarkitekt eller enterprisearkitekt, med fokus på informationsstruktur.
* Minst 2 års erfarenhet från bank- eller försäkringssektorn.
* Tidigare erfarenhet som konsult.
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift.
* Certifieringar inom relevanta områden är meriterande, liksom erfarenhet som verksamhetsarkitekt.


**Vi erbjuder:**
* Attraktiv och flexibel lönemodell.
* Möjlighet att påverka dina arbetstider och arbetsplats.
* Tillgång till utbildningar och certifieringar för din professionella utveckling.
* En stark wellness-satsning genom Team B3, som främjar både hälsa och arbetsglädje.


**Varför B3** 
 Visab? Vi är en del av B3 Consulting Group, ett av Sveriges snabbast växande företag inom IT och management. Hos oss får du det bästa av två världar: kraften från ett stort bolag och den personliga samhörigheten från ett mindre. Vi är stolta över att ha blivit erkända som "Årets Karriärföretag" och av "Great Place to Work". Våra medarbetare pratar om friheten att påverka sina uppdrag, arbetsmiljön där olikheter välkomnas, och stoltheten över att bidra till både kunders framgång och samhället i stort. Vi samarbetar med initiativ som Women in Tech, Changers Hub och Pink Programming för att göra skillnad.
   

  

**Kontakt** 
 :
   

  

 Är du redo att ta nästa steg i din karriär? Ansök nu! Har du frågor är du välkommen att kontakta Lidia Hagos (lidia.hagos@b3.se) eller konsultchef Hanna Skoog (+4670-260 12 65).

title: API-management & API-säkerhets specialist
description:
**Bakgrund**
 Ett av Svenska kraftnäts strategiska projekt har som mål att etablera API-management och behöver kompetensförstärkning. Svenska kraftnät söker en konsult med kompetens för etablering av API management plattform.
   

  

**Uppdragsbeskrivning**
 Vi söker en specialist med kompetens att etablera API-management plattform med hög grad av API säkerhet, kompetensområdet tangerar även IAM (Identity Access Management) och PKI (Public Key Infrastructure). Konsulten kommer arbeta med hela införandet av nyetablering av API-management, från krav, design och tekniskt införande och verifiering, framtagning av teknisk dokumentation och instruktioner, samt kompetensspridning inom säker API-utveckling till mottagande förvaltning. Vi söker dig som har egen praktisk erfarenhet av att etablera API-management plattform (ej molnbaserad) med ”zero trust”-arkitektur med stort fokus på API-säkerhet.
   

  

 Arbete drivs i projektform bestående av projektledare, arkitekter och utvecklare, i nära samarbete med beställare och övrig IT-förvaltning.
   

  

**I uppdraget ingår följande arbetsuppgifter:**
* Du är sakkunnig inom API-säkerhet och kan omsätta behov till tekniska krav, säkerhetskrav, processkrav och lösningsalternativ för API-plattform och konkreta API-implementationer och vägleda i alternativa lösningars för- och nackdelar.
* Du arbetar med handgripligen med installation, konfiguration och härdning
* Du specificerar och implementerar autentiserings- och auktoriseringslösningar för olika APIanvändningsfall (OAuth2.0, OpenID Connect, JWT)
* Du deltar i att utforma säkerhetskontroller som t.ex. rate limiting, och loggning/övervakning av anomalier.
* Du specificerar behov av verifikation och utför verifikationer av lösningen (till exempel med hjälp av OWASP ramverk)
* Du deltar i att ta fram krav och utvärdera plattformar och lösningar i upphandling av API managementplattform.
* Du deltar i att ta fram krav och lösningsförslag som berör IAM och PKI för att implementera API-management plattformslösning.
* Du deltar i hot- och riskanalyser (Threat Modeling) för att identifiera sårbarheter i lösningsförslag
* Du deltar i arbetet med att sätta upp CI/CD-pipelines för automatiserad deployment
* Du deltar i att ta fram krav och processer för API management (t.ex. API onboarding och behörighetshantering).
* Du deltar i att ta fram processer och rutiner för säker API-utveckling (t.ex. policy utveckling, API riktlinjer för API utvecklare

title: Senior Backend Developer
description:
**Senior Backend Developer/Engineer/AI-Something - App 📱** 




  





 ⚠️
 **Den här annonsen kommer ligga ute under sommaren, och vi gillar vår semester så återkoppling kan komma att ske i slutet av augusti** 
 ⚠️
 



  





 Hej, jag heter Niklas och är CTO på BookBeat, kul att du är intresserad av att koda med mig!
 



 Ursäkta den konstiga titeln, men vi är mitt i en resa där vi efter att under förra året blivit duktiga på hur vi jobbar med olika modeller praktiskt nu måste fundera på hur rollen som utvecklare kommer förändras och hur det påverkar oss och andra. Bredare och djupare kunskapsområden, ökat samarbete och kommunikation är vår bästa gissning nu.
 



  





 Jag tror att du har arbetat minst 6 år som backend och har insett att för att kunna ta nästa steg så behöver du koda i ett skickligt team på ett produktbolag där det man gör är på riktigt och har stor inverkan. I rollen som backendutvecklare i appteamet får du möjligheten att arbeta i det team som utvecklar BookBeats yttre publika api och bygger många av funktionerna som finns i appen.
 



  





 Det är viktigt för dig med den mänskliga kontakten och du trivs bäst när du kan bolla idéer, lösa problem och förstå andra team utan ett tangentbord emellan, även om du uppskattar flexibilitet när det behövs. Vi kör 4/1 med en valfri remote-dag och vi försöker vara flexibla när livet händer.
 



 Mitt mål är att bygga små team, med hög kompetens som kan röra sig snabbt med utvecklare som söker ansvar och har en stor påverkan på vad vi bygger och hur vi jobbar både inom och utanför teamet.
 



  





 Med över 1 miljon användare i 30 länder är lasten allt annat än trivial och det ställer stora krav på prestanda och skalbarhet. Det gör arbetet väldigt omväxlande och utmanande. I korthet kan man beskriva BookBeats kultur som startup-lik med få inslag av "corporate" även om det till viss del behövs med 170 anställda. Ekonomiskt står vi på egna ben och är lönsamma med Bonnier som ägare.
 



  





 Jag vill att du är väldigt nyfiken, inte bara på teknik och kod, utan även på hur och varför saker görs. Det kan handla om allt ifrån teknik och domän till människor, team och organisation. Du förstår att det är helheten som spelar roll i slutändan.
 



  





 Du förstår att man måste vara öppen för nya idéer och dela med sig av sina kunskaper med andra utvecklare och specialister för att både BookBeat och du själv ska kunna utvecklas. Jag tror också du är engagerad i det du gör och i ditt yrke, du bryr dig på riktigt om det som du och ditt team skickar till produktion och vilken effekt det ger.
 



  





 Tillsammans är vårt gemensamma mål på BookBeat att bygga Europas bästa tjänst för att hitta och uppleva digitala böcker.
 



  





 Vårt kontor ligger på Sveavägen 56, typ mitt emot Webhallen.
 



  





**Rollen innebär bland annat att:** 



* Skriva kod, bygga nya features
* Produktutveckling
* Förbättra kod
* Delaktighet i arkitektur och tekniska vägval



  





**Jag tror att du är grym på:** 



* C# eller annat OOP språk
* Svenska och engelska



  





**Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av:** 



* Humor
* DDD
* Microtjänstarkitektur
* Meddelandedriven / eventbaserad arkitektur
* Systemdesign och arkitektur
* Coding with agents / AI assisterad utveckling
* Kubernetes
* CI/CD
* REST / gRPC
* Olika databaser
* Azure eller annan cloud-plattform



  





 Har du några frågor om BookBeat eller rollen så kontakta mig på
 
 niklas.arbin@bookbeat.com
 
 .
 



  





**Som anställd på BookBeat AB erbjuds du ett förmånspaket som inkluderar:** 




 🌴 30 dagar semester per år
 



 💰 ITP1-tjänstepension
 



 💌 TGL (tjänstegrupplivförsäkring), TFA (trygghetsförsäkring vid arbetsskada) & tjänstereseförsäkring
 



 🎾 Årligt friskvårdsbidrag
 



 🤸 Veckovis friskvårdstimme
 



 💻 Arbetsutrustning (dator och telefon) samt abonnemang
 



 🎧 BookBeat prenumeration
 



 🏥Företagshälsovård för att säkerställa ditt välmående
 



 👶Föräldraledighetsförmån för att stödja din växande familj
 



 📃Kollektivavtal
 



  





**Utöver våra förmåner erbjuder vi dig också:** 




 💜 En stabil och hållbar balans mellan arbete och fritid. Vi använder flextid och jobbar 37,9 timmar per vecka
 



 🏢 Ett kontor i centrala Stockholm
 



 🏃 … inklusive tillgång till ett fullt utrustat gym med bastu! Vi arrangerar också ett gäng roliga aktiviteter med koppling till hälsa och välmående, såsom vår egna springklubb
 



 🚀 Möjligheten att växa i din roll och karriär, inklusive utmanande och lärorika arbetsuppgifter samt andra möjligheter till utveckling
 



 ✨ Roliga aktiviteter tillsammans med dina kollegor! På BookBeat får du ta del av veckovisa "Friday afternoon mingles", månatliga företagsfrukostar och andra härliga arbetsrelaterade aktiviteter regelbundet tillsammans med hela företaget
 



 🧪 Möjligheten att utforska projekt du inte har tid för i ditt dagliga arbete under våra Hack Days
 



 🧠 Kunskapsdelningsmöjligheter, exempelvis under våra veckovisa "Friday demos" tillsammans med hela företaget där olika team delar vad de har jobbat på, jobbar på eller kommer jobba på. Kunskapsdelning är också en viktig del av vårt dagliga arbete!
 



 🤛 (Sist men inte minst) ett härligt och kompetent team och chef som kommer stötta dig genom din anställning!
 



  





**Om oss** 




 År 2015 påbörjade vi vår resa mot målet att erbjuda Europas ledande tjänst för digitala böcker, och 2016 lanserade vi till allmänheten. Nu, 10 år efter vår breda lansering, används BookBeat av över 1 miljon betalande användare varje månad. Vi är ett ständigt växande företag inom Bonnierkoncernen och alla medlemmar som är del av vår resa har möjlighet att påverka vår utveckling – både på kort och lång sikt.
 



  





 Idag består Team BookBeat av runt 170 nyfikna och engagerade "doers". Är du intresserad av att bli en del av vårt team? Underbart! Våra anställda är experter på många olika områden, men vi har fyra kärnvärderingar som enar oss alla:
 



 🚀 Var fantastisk på det du gör
 



 📣 Dela det du vet och kan
 



 🔎 Nyfikenhet för våra användare och oss
 



 🤝 Bygg Team BookBeat
 



  





 Med andra ord: vi vill att våra medarbetare kontinuerligt ska djupdyka i och utvecklas inom sina kompetensområden, men också att de delar den kunskapen med sina kollegor – vi är alla del av Team BookBeat och samarbetar (oftast mycket) dagligen! Vi söker också medarbetare som är genuint nyfikna på våra användare samt på vår resa och organisation, en nyfikenhet som driver oss till att alltid testa nya saker i vår tjänst och att låta våra användare visa oss om vi är på rätt väg. Sist men definitivt inte minst vill vi att våra anställda ska bidra till Team BookBeats vardagliga samarbete och välmående – som redan nämnt är vi alla del av ett större team, ett team som vi vill ska må bra och ha kul på jobbet, något vi alla bidrar till på olika sätt.

title: Senior Backend Engineer Java
description:
**Introduction**
 IBM Consulting passar dig som vill fortsätta utvecklas tekniskt, men i en miljö där det finns struktur, skala och riktiga problem att lösa för att hjälpa våra kunder och deras verksamheter att lyckas. Du får fantastiska möjligheter till fortbildning och certifieringar, tex inom AI och cloud.
   

  

 Du får jobba i ett stort internationellt bolag, men i uppdrag som är lokalt förankrade och ofta samhällsnära. Det finns gott om kompetens runt dig, samtidigt som du förväntas bidra själv – både tekniskt och i hur teamet jobbar.
   

  

 Och kanske viktigast: du får vara med där klassisk backendutveckling växer ihop med AI på riktigt.
   

  

**Your Role And Responsibilities**
 Vi letar efter dig som har varit med ett tag, som kan backend på riktigt och som trivs i miljöer där systemen är stora, kraven höga och lösningarna behöver hålla över tid.
   

  

 Just nu stärker vi på IBM Consulting upp vårt team i Stockholm med erfarna backendutvecklare till framför allt offentlig sektor, där du kan vara med att bygga och vidareutveckla system som gör skillnad i samhället – varje dag.
   

  

 Som backendutvecklare på IBM Consulting kommer du att jobba tillsammans med andra IBM-kollegor och/eller våra kunder med att utveckla säkra och långsiktigt hållbara lösningar i komplexa IT-miljöer.
   

  

 Typiskt arbetar du i agila team som har helhetsansvar för sitt område och hanterar allt från kravanalys, planering, implementering till verifiering och testning. Du bidrar med din tekniska kompetens, dessutom läggs stor vikt vid din förmåga att hela tiden förbättra arbetssätt och flöden, tex genom automatisering och AI-lösningar. Arbete på plats hos kund på annan ort kan förekomma.
   

  

**Vem Vi Söker**
* Du en nyfiken lagspelare som gärna guidar juniora kollegor.
* Du trivs med kundkontakt.
* Du är trygg i din kompetens och har jobbat några år med backendutveckling, gärna i större organisationer.
* Du gillar teknik och att förstå helheten – hur systemen hänger ihop och varför de byggs som de gör.


**Required Technical And Professional Expertise**
 Vi tror att du har:
   

  

* flera års erfarenhet av backendutveckling (gärna 5+ år)
* stark kompetens inom Java och Spring
* erfarenhet av att bygga och arbeta med API:er (REST)
* kompetens inom arkitektur och designmönster
* vana av att jobba med databaser, både SQL och NoSQL
* erfarenhet av applikationsutveckling i cloud (Azure, AWS eller GCP)
* erfarenhet av agila metoder och arbetssätt
* flytande svenska och engelska i tal och skrift
* rätt att arbeta i Sverige utan restriktioner vid tiden för ansökan


**Preferred Technical And Professional Experience**
 Och gärna också:
   

  

* erfarenhet av AI/ML eller datadrivna lösningar
* erfarenhet av unit-testning (t.ex. JUnit)
* erfarenhet av CI/CD och verktyg som Jenkins
* erfarenhet av Git och moderna utvecklingsflöden

title: Senior Microsoft Fabric-utvecklare
description:
På itm8 fortsätter vi att växa inom Data & AI – och nu söker vi en
 **senior Microsoft Fabric-utvecklare** 
 som vill vara en nyckelperson i våra kunders datadrivna transformation. Du kommer att arbeta i avancerade miljöer där Fabric, Azure och moderna dataarkitekturer står i fokus.
   

  

 Som senior i teamet får du både ta ett tekniskt ledarskap och själv vara hands-on i utvecklingen av datapipelines, Lakehouses, notebooks, integrationer, datamodeller och Fabric-baserade analyslösningar.
   

  

 Här får du jobba tillsammans med några av Nordens bästa experter inom Fabric, BI och Azure – på riktigt spännande och komplexa uppdrag.
   

  

**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:**
* Rådgivning, best practice och tekniskt ledarskap i kunduppdrag
* Delta i arkitektur- och designbeslut för dataplattformar
* Samarbete med BI-utvecklare, analytiker och lösningsarkitekter
* Utveckla och optimera datalösningar i Microsoft Fabric
* Bygga pipelines, Lakehouses, Dataflow Gen2 och integrera datakällor
* Arbeta med Spark, SQL, DAX, notebooks och ETL-flöden
* Utveckla end-to-end-lösningar från ingestion till rapportering


**Erfarenhet och kompetenser:**
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta i utvecklingsteam med DevOps- eller agila metoder
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av kundnära konsultarbete
* Förmåga att ta tekniskt ansvar i projekt
* Förmåga att driva teknisk utveckling och vara mentor
* Stark problemlösningsförmåga
* Meriterande gällande certifieringar så som, DP-600 Fabric Analytics Engineer Associate, Azure Data Engineer Associate samt Power BI Data Analyst Associate
* God kommunikativ förmåga i både svenska och engelska


**Creating the Difference - today, tomorrow, together**
 itm8 är en av de ledande nordiska aktörerna inom specialistconsulting och managerade IT-tjänster för privata företag och den offentliga sektorn. Tillsammans säkerställer vi de bästa tjänsterna för att bli Nordens ledande digitala transformationspartner inom Cloud, Infrastructure & Security, Digital Transformation och Application Services. Läs gärna mer om vår story, kultur och våra erbjudanden på www.itm8.se. Du är också varmt välkommen att lära känna oss mer på Instagram @itm8inside.
   

  

**Redo att utmana(s) med itm8?**
 Varmt välkommen att ansöka till tjänsten nedan. Om du inte är redo att ansöka än och har frågor, skriv till oss i meddelanderutan nedan så följer vi upp med dig. Alla ansökningar och kontakter behandlas konfidentiellt. Vi kontaktar kandidater löpande så vi rekommenderar att du ansöker till tjänsten så snart du kan. I rekryteringsprocessens slutskede genomför vi en obligatorisk och kvalitativ bakgrundskontroll inför anställningsbeslut.

title: Tech Lead Engineer - Connectivity / Infotainment
description:
##### **Project description**

DXC Luxoft is a leading global software services company, delivering professional services across multiple business verticals such as finance, automotive, and digital transformation.  

  

The Automotive practice inside DXC Luxoft delivers software projects to a majority of the world's car manufacturers and suppliers for both personal and commercial vehicles. We are known for our Automotive software expertise and for helping our customers with high quality services and deliveries.  

Currently we are growing for our Swedish customers and are looking for Automotive scripting engineers with knowledge in build system to join our team. You will be part of our growing organization in Sweden and will be part of developing next generation systems for the Automotive industry in the era of connected, autonomous, electric vehicles.


##### **Responsibilities**


We are seeking a skilled Infotainment Tech Lead to join our team and contribute to the development and validation of complex embedded systems within the Automotive Infotainment domain. The ideal candidate will be also familiar with automated testing using Python and PyTest, with a solid understanding of Linux environments and CI/CD workflows.


Key Responsibilities:


Develop and integrate automotive software features across infotainment and connectivity systems


Translate system requirements and architecture into working software solutions


Collaborate closely with system architects, testers, and cross-functional teams


Drive feature development through the V-model lifecycle (design implementation validation)


##### **Skills**


Must have


Education & Experience:


Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field


At least 5 years of experience in software development, with multiple years specifically in embedded Android development (Native C/C++ and Java)


Proven experience in Android development for automotive or mobile platforms, including OS Porting and Bring-Up


Technical Skills — Android:


Deep understanding of Android System Architecture (AOSP level)


Hands-on experience with Android SDK, NDK, JNI, and HIDL


Familiarity with Android Application Framework and Design Patterns (MVP, MVVM)


Knowledge of Android Automotive OS and its specific platform requirements


Understanding of Android platform procedures (build system, OTA, HAL layers, etc.)


Technical Skills — Embedded & Automotive:


Experience in automotive software development within embedded environments


Knowledge of AUTOSAR concepts and their role in automotive software stacks


Experience with tools such as CarWeaver / SystemWeaver for requirement and system design


Infotainment system knowledge


Architecture & Design:


Strong understanding of system architecture and software integration across hardware and software layers


Ability to create and maintain Requirement Specifications and Functional Design documents


Tooling & Process:


Proficient with Jira (project tracking), Gerrit (code review), and Android Studio (development)


Knowledge of Software Planning Methods (e.g., agile, iterative planning) and ability to apply them effectively


Communication & Documentation:


Communicates clearly in English (fluent), both verbally and in writing


Produces accurate, thorough test documentation and reports


Collaboration & Leadership:


Able to work effectively within cross-functional, fast-paced teams


Capable of working independently with minimal supervision


Able to lead and coordinate a team of 5–7 engineers


Quality & Mindset:


Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills


Detail-oriented, organized, and proactive in driving quality improvements


Nice to have


Driving License


##### **Other**


Languages


English: C2 Proficient


Seniority


Regular


  
Goeteborg, Sweden


Req. VR-123429

Automotive Industry


25/06/2026

title: System Engineer - Heat Transfer Equipment
description:
What we do at Blykalla
  
At Blykalla, we're transforming the future of nuclear power by developing innovative Small Modular Reactors (SMRs). Our mission is to create a new generation of nuclear energy systems that are safer, more efficient, and environmentally friendly. As Sweden's only developer of SMRs, we're at the forefront of a groundbreaking industry.
  
The role
  
You will take ownership of the steam generator - one of the most critical components in our SEALER reactor, sitting at the interface between the lead-cooled primary circuit and the power conversion system. You are the system-responsible engineer: defining what it must do, how it should be designed, and who builds it.
  
The role spans the full engineering lifecycle — from writing requirements and leading trade-off studies, through detailed design, manufacturing coordination, and qualification. You will combine sharp mechanical engineering judgment with the ability to align people, suppliers, and analyses toward a common technical goal.
  
What you'll get to do
  
Your work spans three interconnected areas.
  
On the requirements and design basis side, you will define the steam generator's functional and design requirements, develop and maintain the design basis documentation, and lead trade-off studies on configuration, materials, and manufacturing approaches.
  
On the mechanical design side, you will drive decisions on component architecture, assembly sequences, weldability, inspectability, and manufacturability — making sure the design is practical to fabricate, transport, install, test, and maintain.
  
On the thermal-hydraulic and structural side, you will not run complex simulations yourself, but you will be the one asking the right questions: defining calculation scopes, providing inputs to analysts, and critically reviewing results. You should be comfortable performing hand calculations to sanity-check outputs and steer design iterations.
  
On the coordination side, you will define the scope and technical content of supplier work packages, evaluate proposals and deliverables from manufacturers, lead design reviews, and keep component development aligned with plant requirements and the project schedule — working across internal teams as well as academic and industrial partners.
  
Who you’ll get to work with
  
The System Engineer - Heat Transfer Equipment sits within our Fluid Systems Engineering team at Blykalla, reporting to Federico Barbano. You will collaborate closely with thermal-hydraulic analysts, mechanical and system engineers and procurement team — as well as external suppliers and research partners.
  
We are a diverse team that is passionate about our work and about Blykalla.
  
Who you are
  
We are looking for a candidate with up to five years of professional experience. There are a few things we think will help you succeed in this role:
  
Degree in mechanical, energy, chemical, nuclear engineering or a closely related discipline
  
A meaningful portion of your experience spent at a heat exchanger or pressure equipment manufacturer - ideally in a role combining thermohydraulic and mechanical design with production engineering and workshop interface
  
Strong working knowledge of heat exchanger mechanical design (ideally steam generators) and applicable design codes and standards such as ASME, PED, or RCC-M
  
Thermal-hydraulic and heat transfer fundamentals sufficient to steer and critically review calculations performed by others - including the ability to perform hand calculations to sanity-check results
  
Comfort writing clear technical specifications, reviewing deliverables critically, and communicating effectively with both internal colleagues and external suppliers
  
Familiarity with CAD tools and engineering software
  
Fluent in English, spoken and written
  
  

Nuclear experience is welcome but not required — candidates from oil and gas, power generation, petrochemical, and similar industries are highly relevant.
  
Meritorious
  
Hands-on experience with steam generators or shell-and-tube heat exchangers
  
Experience with FEA or CFD — the priority is the ability to define, commission, and review such analyses rather than execute them
  
Prior experience coordinating multidisciplinary design work or managing supplier technical interfaces
  
Background in the nuclear industry
  
  

If you are not sure that you are 100% qualified but are up for the challenge, we encourage you to apply.
  
Location
  
This position is based at our office in Stockholm.
  
So, what do you think?
  
Take the chance to be at the forefront of revolutionizing nuclear power. Join and help us shape the energy landscape of tomorrow. Apply today with your resume and a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and achievements.
  
We offer competitive compensation aligned with our internal salary framework and growth potential in the role.

title: Full-Stack Engineer - Survey & Design
description:
10% of Europe’s CO₂ emissions are caused by residential heating and energy bills are out of control. Aira is here to change that.
  
With our intelligent Aira Heat Pump at the heart, we’re making the switch to clean energy-tech easier and more affordable. Helping Europe turn off gas and slashing energy costs one home at a time.
  
Founded in 2022, and with 1200+ employees and counting, Aira is driving the clean energy revolution by providing homes with modern clean energy-tech solutions such as solar power, battery storage solutions and heat pumps. With a state-of-the-art production facility in Poland, and HQ in Stockholm, Sweden, and hubs across the UK, Germany and Italy, Aira is well on track to install clean energy-tech into 5 million homes by 2034.
  
  

About the role
  
As a Full-Stack Engineer in our Survey & Design team, you'll help build the products that support some of the most important steps in Aira's customer acquisition journey.
  
The team is responsible for the tools used to capture and manage information about our customers' homes, supporting everything from home surveys and technical assessments to installation design. These products help ensure that the right information is available throughout the process, enabling tailored and efficient clean energy solutions.
  
You'll join a small, autonomous product team and work closely with product managers, designers and engineers to solve real-world challenges and build scalable solutions that support Aira's continued growth across Europe. As a Full-Stack Engineer, you'll play a key role in shaping the products and services that power these workflows. You'll work across the stack to build scalable solutions and great user experiences.
  
  

In this role, you will
  
  

Design and build applications and services that support users across multiple European markets
  
  

Develop scalable APIs and integrations with third-party systems
  
  

Process and manage data collected throughout the Survey & Design journey
  
  

Transform complex technical and geometric data into solutions that support our products and workflows
  
  

Contribute to architectural decisions and the long-term evolution of the platform
  
  

Collaborate closely with product managers, designers and end users to improve workflows, user experiences and product capabilities
  
  

Explore new technologies and approaches that help solve real business problems
  
  

We're making home energy design smarter, simpler and more scalable. If you enjoy building great products and solving complex problems in a technically challenging domain, this role is for you.
  
  

What you'll bring to the team
  
  

Around 5+ years of experience as a Software Engineer
  
  

Experience building modern software applications across different parts of the stack
  
  

Strong experience with Java and Spring Boot
  
  

Experience designing and building APIs
  
  

A solid understanding of databases, software architecture and distributed systems
  
  

Experience working with microservices
  
  

The ability to turn ideas into clean, testable and scalable software
  
  

A proactive mindset and willingness to take ownership
  
  

Strong collaboration and communication skills
  
  

We believe you'll thrive in this role if you're curious, analytical and enjoy understanding the real-world challenges behind the software you build. You don't need a background in engineering, but an interest in technical domains and complex problem-solving will help you succeed.
  
We're also looking for someone who enjoys learning new technologies and is curious about how emerging technologies, including AI, can create value for both products and ways of working.
  
Our tech stack
  
Java, Spring Boot, PostgreSQL, Kafka, gRPC, Kotlin, Python, React, TypeScript, Next.js, Kubernetes and Docker.
  
  

What is in it for you?
  
  

Join a mission-driven company accelerating the transition to clean energy across Europe
  
  

Work on products that have a direct impact on how clean energy solutions are designed and delivered
  
  

Be part of a small, highly autonomous team with significant influence on product and technical decisions
  
  

Collaborate with talented colleagues across product, engineering and operations
  
  

Grow professionally in a fast-moving and international environment
  
  

Enjoy our brand-new office at Norra Stationsgatan 93 in Stockholm. Conveniently located near public transport, with Sankt Eriksplan and Odenplan stations just a few minutes walk away.
  
  

At Aira, we champion next-generation energy as well as diversity and inclusion. We firmly believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and creativity, enhancing our ability to serve customers and communities effectively. We welcome individuals from all backgrounds, cultures and perspectives to join our team. Our commitment is to uphold equality and inclusivity, fostering a collaborative environment where everyone can thrive and contribute to a better future for our people and the planet.

title: Senior Microwave Engineer
description:
**Position Summary:**  

We are looking for a Senior Microwave Design Engineer to join our Microwave and Electronics group within the Engineering organization. In this role, you will contribute to the design and development of advanced radar-based sensing technologies used in industrial automation. You will work across the full product lifecycle, helping deliver high-performance, reliable solutions that support safer and more sustainable operations globally. This position is based in Linköping, Sweden, with a hybrid work arrangement available.
**In This Role, Your Responsibilities Will Be:*** Design, develop, and improve passive and active microwave solutions, including radar front-ends, antennas, waveguide structures, and RF components
* Contribute to all phases of product development, from concept and prototyping to testing and production ramp-up
* Perform simulation, design, and validation of RF circuits and systems using industry-relevant tools and methods
* Develop and enhance engineering processes, tools, and ways of working
* Collaborate across multidisciplinary teams to ensure system integration and performance
* Provide technical guidance and share expertise with colleagues across teams
* Engage with external partners, including industry stakeholders and academic institutions, to support innovation and knowledge sharing

**Who You Are:**  

You build collaboration by bringing people together to share ideas and solve problems. You apply your technical expertise to develop practical and innovative solutions, while continuously seeking ways to improve processes and outcomes. You communicate effectively with diverse stakeholders and adapt your approach to different situations. You prioritize work efficiently, focusing on what drives the greatest impact.
**For This Role, You Will Need:*** A degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field, with a focus on high-frequency circuits, systems, or electromagnetics
* Experience working in microwave, RF, or radar system design within a product development or engineering environment
* Hands-on experience with circuit simulation, schematic design, and PCB layout tools (e.g., Microwave Office, HFSS, or similar)
* Practical experience setting up and performing laboratory measurements for RF circuits and systems
* Ability to communicate effectively in English in a global working environment

**Preferred Qualifications That Set You Apart:*** Experience working with FMCW radar technology or similar sensing systems
* Knowledge of antenna design and wireless communication systems
* Familiarity with system integration in industrial or embedded environments
* Experience collaborating across teams or mentoring colleagues
Basic knowledge of Swedish or willingness to learn  
* 

  

**Additional Information:**

This position is based in Linköping and travels to the Gothenburg office will be required when needed. We offer the possibility of working from home one day per week.


If you have any questions or want to know more about us, please contact Niklas Penndal, Director Electrical & Microwave Engineer, at niklas.penndal@emerson.com


Welcome with your application no later than August 9th, 2026. Please note due to summer vacation period you will not hear back from us until August.


Kindly note that due to GDPR, we only accept applications through our career site.


No agencies or calls, please!


**Our Culture and Commitment To You:**  

At Emerson, we are guided by a shared purpose: driving innovation that makes the world healthier, safer, smarter, and more sustainable. We foster an environment where diverse perspectives are valued, and where collaboration and continuous learning are encouraged.
We invest in your development through training, mentorship, and opportunities to grow your career. Our inclusive culture ensures that every employee feels respected, supported, and empowered to contribute. We are committed to creating a workplace where you can do your best work while maintaining balance and well-being.
  
**WHY EMERSON**

**Our Commitment to Our People**

At Emerson, we are motivated by a spirit of collaboration that helps our diverse, multicultural teams across the world drive innovation that makes the world healthier, safer, smarter, and more sustainable. And we want you to join us in our bold aspiration.


We have built an engaged community of inquisitive, dedicated people who thrive knowing they are welcomed, trusted, celebrated, and empowered to solve the world’s most complex problems — for our customers, our communities, and the planet. You’ll contribute to this vital work while further developing your skills through our award-winning employee development programs. We are a proud corporate citizen in every city where we operate and are committed to our people, our communities, and the world at large. We take this responsibility seriously and strive to make a positive impact through every endeavor.


At Emerson, you’ll see firsthand that our people are at the center of everything we do. So, let’s go. Let’s think differently. Learn, collaborate, and grow. Seek opportunity. Push boundaries. Be empowered to make things better. Speed up to break through. Let’s go, together.


**Accessibility Assistance or Accommodation**

If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, please contact: idisability.administrator@emerson.com .  



**ABOUT EMERSON**

Emerson is a global leader in automation technology and software. Through our deep domain expertise and legacy of flawless execution, Emerson helps customers in critical industries like life sciences, energy, power and renewables, chemical and advanced factory automation operate more sustainably while improving productivity, energy security and reliability.


With global operations and a comprehensive portfolio of software and technology, we are helping companies implement digital transformation to measurably improve their operations, conserve valuable resources and enhance their safety.


We offer equitable opportunities, celebrate diversity, and embrace challenges with confidence that, together, we can make an impact across a broad spectrum of countries and industries. Whether you’re an established professional looking for a career change, an undergraduate student exploring possibilities, or a recent graduate with an advanced degree, you’ll find your chance to make a difference with Emerson. Join our team – let’s go!


**No calls or agencies please.**

title: Mechatronics/System Engineer - Seat Belts
description:
**We’re looking for a Mechatronics/System Engineer - Seat Belts for a company in Gothenburg.** **Start is in October, 15 months limited contract to begin with. This role is 100% onsite in Gothenburg.**

**About the Role**


We are strengthening the Seat Belt Team and are looking for a committed and driven mechatronic engineer who wants to contribute to the development of advanced seat belt systems. You will be part of a cross-functional team responsible for system development, quality and program deliveries.

**Key Responsibilities:**


o Team go-to person for Mechatronics (mechanics, electronics, and software)


o Define and maintain system requirements, function logic, and performance target


o Ensure robust system performance (EMC, ELP, electrical behaviour, failure modes, diagnostics)


o Drive supplier performance (timing, quality, technical) and secure commitments


o Collaborate cross-functionally across interfaces, purchasing, manufacturing etc


o Own function, performance, quality, cost, and deliver


o Proactively follow up, resolve deviations, and contribute to continuous improvement and our safety strategy


Who are you / Do you fit the profile?


o You combine strong drive, curiosity, structure, and leadership


o Have a solid understanding of mechatronic systems (mechanics, electronics, and software) and how they interact


o Ability to balance technical depth with delivery focus.


o You take ownership and proactively drive progress


o You ensure effective collaboration across attribute areas, interfaces and with supplier


o Confidently challenge suppliers on technical solutions and system performance


o MSc or BSc in Engineering (preferably Mechatronics, Electrical), or equivalent experience


o Fluent in English, both written and spoken


o Experience in product development, preferably within automotive or safety systems


o Good understanding of Product Development System and engineering ways of working (meritorious)


o Experience in seat belt or restraint systems development (meritorious)


o Knowledge in system/admin tools (meritorious)


o KDP, TCPLM, TeamCenter, Pecca/PCT, Lotus....

**Who are we?**

* Since 1927, we have been known for our commitment to safety. Today, we continue to push boundaries, developing innovative technologies that make life less complicated and safer for our customers.
* Seat Belt Team is part of the Occupant Safety department within Interior. The team is responsible for the development of seat belt systems, one of the most critical safety functions in the vehicle.
* We primarily collaborate with Full-Service Suppliers (FSS) with a global footprint and work closely with engineering teams across regions. Together, we ensure robust development, performance, and delivery of future seat belt technologies

**What’s in It for You?**

* Would you like to work with one of core values—safety—and take on challenges in an international, high-performing environment?
* A key role in developing future seat belt systems within a safety-critical domain
* Part of Seat Belt Team with focus on performance, collaboration, and innovation
* Real impact through leadership, initiative, and driving change
* Continuous learning and growth while shaping world-leading safety

  

This is a full-time consultant position in Gothenburg through Incluso. Start is October, 15 months limited contract to begin with. This role is 100% onsite in Gothenburg.  

  

Please submit your CV as soon as possible since we will review the applications on an on-going basis.


For more information about this role, please contact Kanika Singhal recruiter at Incluso.

title: Senior QA Engineer [Fixed Term]
description:
* Avalanche Studios Group are looking for a skilled Senior QA Engineer for our successful title theHunter: Call of the Wild.
* As Senior QA Engineer, you will be responsible for managing the holistic quality of a robust, live title, maintaining and executing test passes, investigating complex and confounding issues, and coordinating with developers across all disciplines to ensure that maintain our quality targets with every release.
* The role is in our Live Operations QA, which focuses on delivering the entire game on time and at quality to all the various folks who depend on it.
* We need a passionate and dynamic team member, whose attitude is backed up by their experience in QA helping to create great quality games, having worked as an experienced QA Engineer on at least one released project.
  

* **What you'll do**
* Collaborating with Production to ensure satisfactory coverage and risk management across an entire release cycle.
* Investigating, triaging, prioritizing, and managing bugs.
* Planning and executing regression testing and release readiness testing.
* Coordinating across disciplines and teams to drive quality results.
* **Who you are**
* 5+ years experience in Video Games Quality Assurance (preferable experience from shipping AA or AAA games).
* Experience from working as embedded QA within a development team.
* Excellent communication skills – must possess excellent English verbal and written communication skills.
* Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
* Knowledge of bug writing and bug tracking software.
* Experience with testing tools and live product(s) with a frequent release cycle.
* Console & PC testing experience.
* **Desired qualifications**
* Able to define QA strategy, approach and execution.
* Passion for games, the industry and next-generation technology across multiple platforms.
* Able to build a detailed test plan that is flexible with ongoing changes in features and verifies builds for full production testing.
*Note: Research shows under-represented groups in the industry might not apply to a job if they don’t check off every requirement. At Avalanche, we’re more about your drive to learn and interest in tackling challenges than what’s on your resume! So don’t stress if you don’t tick every box— shoot us your application anyway! Diverse opinions and different experiences are what make our teams so great, not itemized lists of qualifications.*
**The Stockholm location**
Our Stockholm office is situated in Södermalm, the beating heart of the city’s game industry. It’s a physical representation of our games – the lush vegetation of Just Cause and theHunter: Call of the Wild meets corroded metal straight out of Mad Max. Our hybrid model requires you to be in the office on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays to foster collaboration and creativity.
**Our values**
At Avalanche Studios Group, we create worlds beyond limits. But that’s not exclusive to our games, so we’re committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. All Avalanchers have a shared responsibility to create an open work environment where everyone is treated equally and respectfully. Being part of our world is not contingent on your heritage, your gender, or sexual orientation. It’s all about your passion and creativity.
**How to apply**
To apply for this position, provide your CV in English. We review applications continuously. All further studio-related information is provided under a non-disclosure agreement.
*At Avalanche Studios Group we're always on the lookout for talented individuals to join our passionate team, but please note that relocation assistance is not available for this role.*
company_description: Avalanche Studios is the original creator of the award-winning Just Cause franchise (published by Square Enix) with the third entry release

title: Automation Engineer - Painting
description:
**Professional Galaxy** is an IT and technology consulting company that provides highly specialized expertise within IT, software development, SAP, purchasing, electronics and mechanical design. We collaborate with experienced senior experts and deliver strategic value-creating expertise to some of Sweden's most complex and analytically demanding projects. Our focus is always on high quality, professionalism and clear, measurable results.


We are now seeking an **Automation Engineer - Painting** - for one of our clients.

**About the assignment:**


We are looking for an experienced Paint Automation Engineer who wants to contribute to the development of a highly automated manufacturing environment at a leading global industrial company.  

The assignment offers a unique opportunity to work close to production while driving the development of automated paint processes in a modern and technology-driven environment. The client is investing in future production capabilities, automation, and digitalization, creating an exciting setting for someone who enjoys combining hands-on problem solving with continuous improvement and long-term development.  

In this role, you will act as the automation specialist within automated paint operations, supporting daily production while contributing to industrialization projects, process optimization, and future automation initiatives. You will collaborate closely with production, maintenance, engineering, and project teams to ensure stable, safe, and efficient operations.  

This assignment is ideal for someone who enjoys taking ownership, solving complex technical challenges, and making a tangible impact in a high-tech manufacturing environment.  

  

Required skills  

We believe you have:  

* A Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Automation, Robotics, or equivalent experience.
* Experience working with industrial automation in manufacturing environments.
* Strong understanding of automated production systems and industrial processes.
* Experience with robot programming and automation systems.
* Ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in complex production environments.
* Experience working with continuous improvements and process optimization.
* Strong communication and collaboration skills.
* The ability to work independently, prioritize effectively, and drive activities forward.
* Fluency in English, both written and spoken.

  

Preferred skills  

* Experience in one or several of the following areas is considered a strong advantage:
* Automated paint processes within industrial manufacturing.
* Robot-based paint applications and process programming.
* Production support in highly automated environments.
* Industrialization and implementation of new production technologies.
* Process optimization related to quality, efficiency, and productivity.
* Automated material handling and manufacturing systems.
* Pneumatic systems and industrial process equipment.
* Working in regulated production environments with high safety requirements.
* Technical leadership or coordination of smaller projects and activities.

  

Personal profile  

We are looking for a curious and engaged professional who enjoys working where technology meets operations.  

You have a structured and analytical approach to problem solving and are motivated by identifying improvements that create long-term value. Collaboration comes naturally to you, and you are comfortable building relationships across different functions and levels within an organization.  

You enjoy sharing knowledge, supporting colleagues, and driving technical development forward. Most importantly, you are someone who takes initiative and sees opportunities where others see challenges.  

If you are passionate about industrial automation and want to contribute to the continued transformation of a modern production environment, we would love to hear from you.**Additional information**  

**Assignment start:** 2026-08-17

**Remote work:** No

**Assignment duration:** 1 year

**Geographical region:** Sweden\Västmanlands län\Stockholms län\Södermanlands län\Uppsala län\Örebro län\Dalarnas län, ,City:Västerås (VÄSTERÅS)

**Reply no later than:** 2026-07-31


Are you the right person for the assignment, or do you want to recommend a strong candidate? Do not hesitate to contact us.


Please apply directly through our system with:

* Your updated CV in english
* Availability to start the assignment


In the motivation, describe why you are suitable for this assignment - refer to previous consulting assignments, employmxent, education and personal qualities.

**Please note:** We do not accept any applications through mail. All applications have to be sent through the portal to be valid.

**Offer continuously:** Please note that for this role we offer continuously. That means that we sometimes remove the assignments before the deadline. If you are interested, we recommend that you apply immediately.

title: Senior Embedded Hardware & Firmware Engineer — Project Based
description:
**About Humans since 1982:**  

  

We are a pioneering studio based in Stockholm, working at the intersection of design, art, and technology. Our kinetic installations, including *A million Times* and *ClockClock*, have found audiences around the world. Among our collectors are institutions such as the Vitra Design Museum, companies including OpenAI, as well as global thought leaders across business and culture.


Our work is guided by a simple idea: **“**Form follows fascination**.”** Curiosity is where every project begins. While the outcomes often take the form of physical objects, we approach them dramaturgically, arranging anticipation, surprise, and resolution.

**About the role**  

  

**What you'll do**

* Redesign and optimize the power architecture to support a large number of synchronized units operating from a shared power system
* Strengthen and scale the communication architecture for a multi-node installation
* Develop, test, and harden embedded firmware for reliable long-term operation
* Diagnose and resolve system-level reliability issues across hardware and software
* Prototype, validate, and refine solutions through hands-on testing
* Bring the system to a production-ready, reproducible state within the project timeline

**Who we think you are**

* You have successfully delivered embedded electronics products from prototype through production
* A strong embedded firmware engineer, ideally with C/C++ on platforms such as STM32, ESP32, or similar MCUs
* Comfortable with hardware bring-up, debugging, and system integration
* A solid understanding of power electronics, motor control, and electromechanical systems
* You thrive in independent, senior-level roles and communicate clearly in a remote working environment


Nice to have: CAN bus or other industrial communication protocols · EMC, electrical safety, or compliance experience · motion-control systems at scale · embedded Linux.

**Engagement**

* Contract / project-based engagement
* Flexible working hours over the next ~6 months, with a minimum of 20 hours per week
* Open to different setups — for example full-time across ~3 months, or 30–60% across ~6 months
* Start date: August–September 2026
* Remote, with a strong preference for on-site time in Stockholm during key development, testing, and integration phases
* Opportunity for additional project work beyond the initial engagement

**What we offer**

* Ownership of a technically ambitious and highly visible project
* Direct collaboration with our technical lead and senior hardware advisor
* Flexible working with overlap across European working hours
* The opportunity to contribute to internationally recognised design and art projects
* A rare working environment at the intersection of engineering, design, and contemporary art

*We review applications on a rolling basis, with interviews from August.*

**Culture at Humans since 1982**


At Humans since 1982, we work in a small but distinctive field where design, art, and engineering meet. Our studio creates collectible pieces that require patience, precision, and a genuine fascination for how things work, unfold and surprise over time.


We are a curious and collaborative team. Everyone in the studio is trusted to take ownership of their work, think independently, and contribute ideas beyond their immediate role. At the same time, our projects are collective efforts, built through dialogue, shared curiosity, and a respect for each other’s craft.


The studio still operates with an entrepreneurial spirit. We are flexible and move quickly when ideas take shape, but we also recognise that meaningful work takes care and attention. As we continue our journey, every person in the team helps shape not only the work we create, but the culture of the studio.

  

Founded in 2009


Co-workers 11

**humanssince1982.com** **/**  

**Instagram: humanssince1982**

title: Senior Technical Architect - SoC, Verilog, UVM, C, C++
description:
Stockholm, Stockholm
  
Job Summary
  
  

Good understanding of ASIC/SoC life cycle Has participated in multiple ASIC/SoC verification till tapeout stage Experience writing ASIC/SoC testplans 1.Experience in ASIC/SoC Testbench definition 2.Experience to Build and maintain reusable block-level and sub-system testbenches using SystemVerilog and the Universal Verification Methodology (UVM). 1.Expertise & hands-on experience in OVM/UVM methodologies using SV 2.Experience to Write, execute, and debug constrained-random and directed test cases based on defined test plans. 1. Experience in developing TB components for SOC with C, SV 1. Experience in developing TB components, including functional coverage implementation and assertion coding 2. Experience Set up functional coverage, write system assertions (SVA), and analyze code coverage metrics to identify untested gaps in the design logic. 3.Debug complex simulation failures using waveform viewer tools to isolate design bugs from testbench issues. 4. Experince in SOC C based tests coding & debugging Experience in Gate level simulation & netlist debugging Exeprince in regression failure analayiss Functioncal and Code Coverage closure Experience in Utilizing scripting languages like Perl/Shell scripting to automate regression tests and parse large simulat
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
1. Lead strategic RTL Design initiatives by architecting complex solutions with Verilog, VHDL, and SystemVerilog, ensuring alignment with enterprise goals and measurable business outcomes.
  
2. Establish governance and quality frameworks for RTL Design projects, utilizing advanced simulation and synthesis tools such as Synopsys Design Compiler and Cadence Genus to mitigate risk and uphold compliance.
  
3. Define and execute solution roadmaps that integrate emerging RTL optimization techniques, driving innovation and scalability for enterprise-wide deployments.
  
4. Oversee multi-disciplinary teams in delivering high-impact RTL Design solutions, mentoring senior technical staff and embedding best practices in digital design and verification.
  
5. Collaborate with executive stakeholders and clients to identify strategic opportunities for RTL-based differentiation, leveraging industry trends and advanced design methodologies.
  
6. Manage large-scale RTL programs, coordinating resource allocation and budget oversight to ensure timely, cost-effective delivery and operational risk management.
  
7. Integrate advanced hardware technologies and IP cores within RTL architectures to support business transformation and future-readiness.
  
Skill Requirements
  
**1. RTL Design:** Enterprise Strategy and Visionary Leadership in Verilog, VHDL, SystemVerilog.
  
2. Solid expertise in RTL synthesis, simulation, timing analysis, and optimization.
  
3. Advanced proficiency in EDA tools such as Synopsys Design Compiler, Cadence Genus, and Mentor Graphics Questa.
  
4. Excellent understanding of digital architecture, SoC integration, and hardware/software co-design.
  
5. Strategic knowledge of design verification, low-power design, and advanced technology nodes.
  
6. Strong leadership in solution governance, risk management, and quality assurance for RTL projects.
  
Other Requirements
  
**1. Optional but valuable:** Certified Design Engineer (CDE), Synopsys Certified Implementation Specialist, Cadence Digital Design Certification.

title: Senior ASIC Verification Lead | Telecom / Networking Semiconductor Domain
description:
**Who We Are**


At MittLogik Group, we’re driven by a passion for solving complex challenges through technology. As a growing IT services provider, we specialize in delivering high-impact consulting and engineering expertise across the Automotive, Embedded Systems, and Telecom industries.


With a strong focus on quality and adaptability, we help our clients accelerate development, scale innovation, and stay ahead in fast-moving technical environments. Our ability to connect niche talent with critical projects has made us a trusted partner to both startups and industry leaders.


Recognized with the DI Gasell award in 2020, 2021, and 2023, we’re proud to be one of Sweden’s fastest-growing companies.


Headquartered in Lund, with teams across Stockholm, Gothenburg, Linköping, and Bangalore, we operate globally with a local mindset.


Explore more at mittlogik.se

**The Challenge**

We are looking for a Senior ASIC Verification Engineer who thrives in a high-performance environment and wants to take true ownership of the design cycle. In this role, you won’t just be a cog in a machine—you will be the gatekeeper of quality, helping our top-tier clients (including Fortune 500 leaders) push the boundaries of 5G, 6G, and next-gen hardware.


You will span the entire verification spectrum: from deep-diving into intricate IP blocks (for massive MIMO, high-speed data processing, and complex radio algorithms) to managing the top-level SoC integration of multi-chip modules featuring hundreds of processor cores, tailored hardware accelerator IPs, and high-speed interfaces.


What You Will Do


Architect Test Environments: Design, build, and maintain advanced, extensible, and modular IP and SoC-level testbenches from scratch using UVM and SystemVerilog.


End-to-End Verification: Define comprehensive test plans, driving verification strategies from block-level logic to top-level ingress/egress traffic involving DSPs, MCUs, switches, and security components.


Bug Hunting & Coverage: Use constrained random testing and Assertion-Based Verification (ABV) to unearth complex corner-case bugs, ensuring 100% functional and code coverage.


Software-Driven Testing: Develop embedded software-driven tests in C to run on embedded processors within the SoC, debugging failing test cases across SystemC/TLM platforms, RTL simulation, and silicon.


Emulation & Automation: Prepare and configure designs for Hardware Emulators (e.g., Palladium, Zebu, Veloce) and maintain continuous integration (CI) regression suites across multiple simulation and emulation platforms.


Collaborate & Mentor: Work closely with RTL designers to close loops on timing and functionality, while mentoring junior engineers and influencing architectural decisions across cross-functional teams.

**Who You Are**


You are a meticulous problem-solver at heart who enjoys the "detective work" of verification as much as technical precision and collaboration.


Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.


Experience: 10+ years of hands-on experience in ASIC/SoC and IP-level verification, ideally within the telecommunications or semiconductor industry.


Methodologies: Expert-level mastery of SystemVerilog and UVM/OVM is mandatory. Practical debugging experience with VHDL or Specman (e) is a strong plus.


Software Skills: Strong C programming skills for embedded software/verification environments, alongside scripting expertise in Python, Perl, or Linux Shell scripting.


Domain Knowledge: Familiarity with digital logic design, mobile communication standards (5G/6G), and standard interfaces (e.g., Ethernet, SGMII, PCIe, CPRI, SPI, I3C).


Soft Skills: Excellent communication skills with an agile mindset and a proven track record of mentoring team members.

**Why MittLogik?**

We believe your work should be something you love. At MittLogik, we offer a vibrant culture where your opinion matters.


Growth & Flexibility: We keep doors open for you to learn new skills, explore different technologies, or pivot to new projects.


Global Mobility: We are an equal-opportunity employer with a diverse, international team. We provide relocation support and visa sponsorship for talent joining us in Sweden.


Vibrant Culture: From quarterly after-work events and games to our legendary summer weekend getaways and festive Christmas dinners, we celebrate our wins together.


Flat Organization: Work with cutting-edge tech in an environment that truly values "freedom and responsibility."

title: Calibration Engineer
description:
**Assignment description**

* Calibration Engineer within the Thermal Management and Hydraulics area for xEV powertrains and vehicles.
* The work scope includes thermal energy coordination between the different elements of a xEV powertrain and comfort of the passenger compartment. This includes controlling the heating and cooling of the combustion engine, e-machine, transmission, power electronics, battery and cabin. Your responsibility is to perform calibration and verification activities on desktop, engine rig or in vehicle to fulfill the attribute targets of the powertrain and vehicle.
* Depending on your background and interest the scope could also include function development of our inhouse application software or to support on topics related to oil pressure control.
* You will also support with system design with future products for a variety of applications to different customers.
* Work will be carried out in both vehicles and in a test cell, testing may also be carried out in locations other than Gothenburg and in climates that are not found in the immediate area.﻿

**Skill requirements**

* Worked with thermal management on applications with combustion or electrical powertrains
* Worked with both calibration and function development using Simulink
* Driving license

title: Customer Quality Engineer
description:
Location:
Gothenburg - Sweden
Job Family:
Quality
Worker Type Reference:
Regular - Permanent
Pay Rate Type:
Salary
Career Level:
P3
Job ID:
R-53490-2026
  
### **Description & Requirements**

**A Career at HARMAN Automotive**  

We’re a global, multi-disciplinary team that’s putting the innovative power of technology to work and transforming tomorrow. At HARMAN Automotive, we give you the keys to fast-track your career.

* Engineer audio systems and integrated technology platforms that augment the driving experience
* Combine ingenuity, in-depth research, and a spirit of collaboration with design and engineering excellence
* Advance in-vehicle infotainment, safety, efficiency, and enjoyment

**About the Role**


The Customer Quality Engineer will work with our OEM customer and extended Harman team to resolve quality issues resulting from design and/or manufacturing discrepancies.  

  

**What You Will Do**

* Work with multifunctional teams (Operations, Manufacturing, Engineering, Field Service, Sales) to drive resolution for product complaints.
* Champion product or component level testing to support failure investigations and determine root cause.
* Conduct/Update risk analyses and track all findings, develop Risk Management portfolio, as required.
* Responsible for field containment and rework activities at customer locations, such as product diagnostic tests, assembly line investigations and assembly plant yard-hold management.
* Support and maintain policies and practices related to manufacturing support of 0km issues at customer assembly plants and quality situations related to product application in vehicles.
* Represent Harman at customer meetings, conferences and training on behalf of the manufacturing plants and effectively deploy the information throughout the organization, ensuring Harman is current with the customer’s quality policies.
* Analyze, compile, review and regularly report customer quality metrics such as PPM or QR for 0km performance, preparing presentations from quality data for reviews by both Harman and customer management groups.
* Obtain customer approvals for manufacturing process change(s) by providing supporting data for the change(s) and submitting to customer in their required format.
* Lead problem analysis and improvement efforts by applying the use of modern quality tools such as FMEA, 8D, Kaizen, Lean Manufacturing principles and Six-Sigma methodologies.
* Ensure information flow related to customer returns to appropriately address design (hardware and software), manufacturing, packaging and logistics causes, and facilitating awareness of issues and deploy countermeasures as lessons learned (Yokoten).
* Support APQP activities with customer and Harman manufacturing to facilitate PPAP submission according to customer schedule.
* Liaison between customer and Harman, coordinating activities of internal and external resources, serving as resource for problem solving by initiating actions to address issues and prevent potential quality problems.
* Assist internal and external contacts to resolve product and process problems, investigating and solving manufacturing-related problems.
* Champion continuous improvement efforts; researches, recommends, implements and administers new and/or existing quality assurance processes.
* Complete other projects and activities as assigned by Customer Quality Leader.

 **What You Need**

* Bachelor’s degree, with a preference for Electrical or Mechanical Engineering.
* 5+ years of experience in automotive quality.
* Knowledge of the principles and procedures related to Manufacturing Quality Engineering, electronic & mechanical manufacturing, statistical process control (SPC), problem solving and automotive production part approval process (PPAP)
* Experience using AIAG guidelines (APQP, PPAP, PFMEA).
* Practical problem solving using: 8D, A3, Fault Tree Analysis, Pareto, 3L-5Y, Is-Is Not, Fish Bone, etc.

 **What is Nice to Have**

* 3+ years of experience with automotive electronics, audio and/or infotainment preferred.
* Familiar with CANALYZER / flash scripting, hardware and / or software experience.
* Master’s Degree in Mechanical, Electrical or Industrial Engineering.
* ISO 14001 and TS 16949 experience.
* Certifications/Licenses: ASQ, CQE, Six Sigma Green Belt.
* Ability to specify, develop and operate specialized equipment used for product analysis.
* Ability to calculate and analyze data, generate reports.

 **What Makes You Eligible**

* Be willing to travel.
* Successfully complete a background investigation as a condition of employment.

 **What We Offer**

* Flexible work environment.
* Access to employee discounts on world-class products (JBL, HARMAN Kardon, AKG, and more).
* Extensive training opportunities through our own HARMAN University.
* Competitive wellness benefits.
* “Be Brilliant” employee recognition and rewards program.
* An inclusive and diverse work environment that fosters and encourages professional and personal development.


#LI-MK1

  


**About HARMAN: Where Innovation Unleashes Next-Level Technology**
Ever since the 1920s, we’ve been amplifying the sense of sound. Today, that legacy endures, with integrated technology platforms that make the world smarter, safer, and more connected.  

  

Across automotive, lifestyle, and digital transformation solutions, we create innovative technologies that turn ordinary moments into extraordinary experiences. Our renowned automotive and lifestyle solutions can be found everywhere, from the music we play in our cars and homes to venues that feature today’s most sought-after performers, while our digital transformation solutions serve humanity by addressing the world’s ever-evolving needs and demands. Marketing our award-winning portfolio under 16 iconic brands, such as JBL, Mark Levinson, and Revel, we set ourselves apart by exceeding the highest engineering and design standards for our customers, our partners and each other.  

If you’re ready to innovate and do work that makes a lasting impact, join our talent community today!  

  

**Important Notice: Recruitment Scams**
Please be aware that HARMAN recruiters will always communicate with you from an '@harman.com', ‘@careers.harman.com’ or ‘@harmanglobal.avature.net’ email address. We will never ask for payments, banking, credit card, personal financial information or access to your LinkedIn/email account during the screening, interview, or recruitment process. If you are asked for such information or receive communication from an email address not ending in one of the above email domains about a job with HARMAN, please cease communication immediately and report the incident to us through: harmancareers@harman.com. **You Belong Here**
HARMAN is committed to making every employee feel welcomed, valued, and empowered. No matter what role you play, we encourage you to share your ideas, voice your distinct perspective, and bring your whole self with you – all within a support-minded culture that celebrates what makes each of us unique. We also recognize that learning is a lifelong pursuit and want you to flourish. We proudly offer added opportunities for training, development, and continuing education, further empowering you to live the career you want.  

  

HARMAN is proud to be an Equal Opportunity employer. HARMAN strives to hire the best qualified candidates and is committed to building a workforce representative of the diverse marketplaces and communities of our global colleagues and customers. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, gender (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. HARMAN attracts, hires, and develops employees based on merit, qualifications and job-related performance.(www.harman.com)  

HARMAN is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities. If you need assistance or an accommodation during the application process, please contact us at harmancareers@harman.com. Requests will be considered on a case-by-case basis in accordance with applicable law.
